description:
A team of FinOps analysts has asked you to organise, archive, and summarise the latest month-end cloud-usage exports that just landed on the workstation.  
  Everything must be done only from the Linux terminal, without elevated privileges.

  1. Starting point  
     • Three raw CSV files already exist in /home/user/incoming_reports/  
       - aws_costs_2023-09.csv  
       - azure_costs_2023-09.csv  
       - gcp_costs_2023-09.csv  

  2. Target directory layout (must be created exactly as shown)  
     /home/user/FinOps/  
       └── 2023/  
           └── 09/  
               ├── aws/  
               │   ├── costs.csv        ← the raw data after being renamed & moved  
               │   └── summary.txt      ← generated by you  
               ├── azure/  
               │   ├── costs.csv  
               │   └── summary.txt  
               ├── gcp/  
               │   ├── costs.csv  
               │   └── summary.txt  
               └── cost_summary_2023-09.log  ← consolidated multi-provider log

  3. Actions you must perform  
     a. Create the folder hierarchy exactly as shown.  
     b. Move each provider’s raw CSV into its matching sub-folder and rename it strictly to costs.csv.  
     c. For every provider folder create a summary.txt file containing ONE single line:  
        TotalCost=<total_to_two_decimals>  
        • The total is the arithmetic sum of the “cost_usd” column in that provider’s costs.csv.  
        • Keep exactly two digits after the decimal point.  
        • No extra spaces, no newline at the end.  
     d. Generate /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/cost_summary_2023-09.log.  
        • The file must have exactly three lines, one per provider, sorted alphabetically by provider name (aws, azure, gcp).  
        • Each line must be valid JSON with this exact schema:  
          {"provider":"<name>","total_cost_usd":<total_to_two_decimals>}  
        • No trailing commas, spaces, or blank lines.  
     e. Set permissions so that every directory is 755 and every file is 644.

  4. Verification requirements (automated grader will check)  
     • The directory structure and file names match 100 % exactly (case-sensitive).  
     • The numeric totals in summary.txt and in cost_summary_2023-09.log are arithmetically correct to two decimals.  
     • cost_summary_2023-09.log lines are in the required order and conform to the JSON schema.  
     • Each summary.txt is a one-liner with no trailing newline.  
     • File and directory permissions are set as specified.

  5. Tips & constraints  
     • You are free to use any shell utilities (cut, awk, paste, find, etc.) but do not hard-code totals. Calculate them programmatically.  
     • The task is multi-step; feel free to chain or parallelise commands where appropriate.  
     • Do all work under /home/user; no sudo is available or required.  
     • Console output is allowed, but only the filesystem artefacts will be graded, so make sure they exactly match the specification above.
truth:
Existing files before the task starts:
    /home/user/incoming_reports/aws_costs_2023-09.csv
      service,cost_usd
      EC2,25.50
      S3,7.25
      Lambda,2.40

    /home/user/incoming_reports/azure_costs_2023-09.csv
      service,cost_usd
      VM,30.00
      Storage,5.75
      Functions,1.10

    /home/user/incoming_reports/gcp_costs_2023-09.csv
      service,cost_usd
      ComputeEngine,18.60
      CloudStorage,6.90
      CloudFunctions,0.95

  Expected totals:
    aws   → 35.15
    azure → 36.85
    gcp   → 26.45

  After successful completion the grader will assert:
    1. Directories:
       /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/aws      (mode 755)
       /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/azure    (mode 755)
       /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/gcp      (mode 755)
    2. Files and permissions:
       /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/aws/costs.csv          (mode 644)
       /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/azure/costs.csv        (mode 644)
       /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/gcp/costs.csv          (mode 644)
       /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/aws/summary.txt        (mode 644, content: TotalCost=35.15)
       /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/azure/summary.txt      (mode 644, content: TotalCost=36.85)
       /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/gcp/summary.txt        (mode 644, content: TotalCost=26.45)
       /home/user/FinOps/2023/09/cost_summary_2023-09.log (mode 644, content exactly):
         {"provider":"aws","total_cost_usd":35.15}
         {"provider":"azure","total_cost_usd":36.85}
         {"provider":"gcp","total_cost_usd":26.45}

  The grader will perform diff-based checks to ensure:
    • No extra files or lines exist.
    • Summary lines do not contain trailing whitespace or newlines.
    • Permissions match exactly.
